brand.tagline: Memphis-inspired React components, tokens, and patterns.
brand.mascotAlt: Damo UI mascot — a purple axolotl wearing glasses
brand.mascotHeroAlt: Damo UI mascot — a purple axolotl coding on a stack of books

home.leadSuffix: Compose React + Next.js UIs without giving up the visual identity.

components.title: 47 components
components.desc: Buttons, cards, dialogs, inputs, and 40+ more, all on a Memphis-styled chrome.

tokens.title: 3-layer tokens
tokens.desc: Raw scales feed semantic pairs that feed identity overrides — switchable live.

themeGenerator.title: Theme Generator
themeGenerator.desc: Visual editor with per-mode palette and CSS / Tailwind / JSON exports.

notFound.heading: Page not found.
notFound.body: That link does not lead anywhere. Head back home, or jump straight into the documentation.

gettingStarted.lead: Damo UI is a React + Next.js component library inspired by Memphis design. Tokens, components, and patterns ready to compose into a product UI.

step2.title: 2. Wire the styles
step2.body: Import the design tokens and the global stylesheet once in your root layout. Tokens define colors, typography, radius, shadow, and motion as CSS variables.
step2.tailwindHint: If you use Tailwind v4, replace your <mono>globals.css</mono> with:

step4.title: 4. Wire theme, palette, density
step4.body: Three orthogonal data-attributes on <html> drive every visual choice. Drop the lib's switcher components in your top bar and you're done — they read & write the attributes and persist to localStorage:
step4.themeBullet: data-theme — the lib ships light only; declare your own [data-theme='dark'] CSS overrides. Wire a <AttrToggleGroup attribute="data-theme"> to switch between values.
step4.paletteBullet: data-palette — no built-ins; the lib's neutral defaults assume a single palette. Define [data-palette='cyberpunk'], [data-palette='sunset'], etc., and pass the same list to <AttrToggleGroup attribute="data-palette">.

gettingStarted.themingHint: Full guide with dark-mode setup, custom palettes, programmatic switching, and FOUC prevention → <link>Theming</link>.
gettingStarted.ctaReady: Ready to compose? Browse <componentsLink>components</componentsLink> or <generatorLink>generate a custom theme</generatorLink>.

tokens.lead: Damo UI uses a three-layer token architecture. Raw scales are private; semantic pairs are the public surface; identity tokens are component-specific overrides. Every token is a CSS variable so theme, palette, and density switch live without rebuilds.

section1.title: 1. Raw palette (private)
section1.body: Numeric scales like --plum-*, --gold-*, --paper-*. These power the semantic layer but are not exposed as Tailwind utilities — using them directly couples your code to brand specifics.

section2.title: 2. Semantic tokens (public)
section2.body: Paired <mono>bg</mono>/<mono>fg</mono> pairs: <mono>--background</mono> + <mono>--foreground</mono>, <mono>--primary</mono> + <mono>--primary-foreground</mono>, etc. This is the layer product code should consume.
section2.consumingBody: Three equivalent ways. Pick the one that fits where you are:

section3.title: 3. Identity tokens
section3.body: Component-specific overrides. They reference the semantic layer but allow narrow adjustments — <mono>--nav-on-dark-*</mono>, <mono>--badge-*</mono>, <mono>--chart-*</mono>. Add your own when a component needs a value the semantic layer doesn't express.

tokens.overrideBody: Any token is just a CSS variable. Override at <mono>:root</mono> to change the whole document, or scope to a wrapper class for an island:
tokens.densityBody: The density attribute flips a single multiplier: <mono>--density-scale-y</mono>. Bake it into your component spacing and density switching becomes free.
tokens.switchingBody: Three switchers live on <mono><html></mono>: <mono>data-theme</mono>, <mono>data-palette</mono>, <mono>data-density</mono>. They are orthogonal — any combination works. See <link>Theming</link> for the full wiring.
tokens.generatorHint: Want to author a custom theme? The <link>Theme Generator</link> edits all three layers visually and exports CSS, Tailwind, or JSON.

theming.lead: Three orthogonal data-attributes on <mono><html></mono> drive every visual choice. Switch one without touching the others, define your own variants on top of the lib's neutral defaults, and persist the user's preference across reloads.
theming.switchersBody: Damo UI reads three independent attributes from the document root. They cascade through every component because the design tokens are CSS variables.
theming.themeCardBody: Toggles surfaces, foreground, and Memphis chrome. The lib ships light only — you declare the dark override.
theming.paletteCardBody: No built-in values — declare any list you want (<mono>default</mono>, <mono>sunset</mono>, <mono>cyberpunk</mono>, <mono>forest</mono>…) and override <mono>--primary</mono> & friends per palette.
theming.densityCardBody: Multiplies vertical spacing via <mono>--density-scale-y</mono>. Lib ships these three values out of the box.
theming.darkBody: Re-declare every public token under <mono>[data-theme='dark']</mono>. You only need to override the semantic layer — components consume those, never the raw scales directly.
theming.darkRefList: The full list of tokens you can override lives in <colorsLink>Colors</colorsLink> (semantic) and <tokensLink>Tokens</tokensLink> (architecture).
theming.customPaletteBody: Same pattern, different attribute. You can scope a palette to a single mode by chaining attribute selectors.
theming.densityScaleBody: Density flips a single CSS variable that components multiply into their vertical padding. You can change the multipliers globally if the defaults don't suit your product.
theming.dropInBody: The lib ships three controls that read & write the attributes and persist to <mono>localStorage</mono>. Pop them anywhere — the navbar is the most common home.
theming.programmaticBody: If the built-in components don't fit (custom UI, settings page, system-preference media query, A/B test…) flip the attributes by hand:
theming.fouBody: Server-rendered apps render the default theme first, then React hydrates the persisted choice. To avoid the flash of unstyled-theme (FOUT), restore the attributes from <mono>localStorage</mono> in a blocking script before hydration.
theming.scopedBody: The attributes work on any element, not only <html>. Wrap a section in data-theme="dark" to flip just that subtree.
theming.tipBody: The <link>Theme Generator</link> authors light + dark variants visually and exports the CSS overrides for steps 1 and 2. Skip the manual hex hunt.

colors.lead: Three brand scales (Plum, Gold, Paper) and twenty+ semantic tokens. Switch the palette in the navbar to see every swatch on this page update live. Use the semantic layer in product code — it re-points automatically when theme or palette changes.

ink.name: Ink
ink.desc: Dark primary — foreground, text, night surfaces

colors.semanticBody: Use these in product code. They re-point automatically when the palette changes.
colors.jsxBody: Every semantic token is exposed as a Tailwind utility: <mono>bg-*</mono>, <mono>text-*</mono>, <mono>border-*</mono>. Pair the bg with its matching foreground for guaranteed contrast.
colors.cssBody: Need a color outside Tailwind's utilities? Read the variable directly.
colors.pairBody: Each background semantic ships with a matching foreground variant. Mixing them is the only way to guarantee contrast across themes and custom palettes.
colors.darkBody: The lib ships light only. Re-declare semantic tokens under [data-theme='dark'] and the whole UI flips when the user toggles theme.
colors.customPaletteBody: Same pattern with <mono>data-palette</mono>. Override only the tokens that should change — everything else inherits from the default palette.
colors.themingHint: Full theming wiring (FOUC prevention, scoped islands, programmatic switching) → <link>Theming</link>.

typography.lead: Audiowide for display, Exo 2 for body and UI. The personality lives in weight, tracking, and the eyebrow mono accent — no extra families needed.
typography.loadingBody: The lib ships var(--font-*) tokens but does not embed font files. Load Audiowide + Exo 2 yourself (Google Fonts link or next/font) and bind the families to the lib's variables.
typography.loadingNextHint: Or use Next.js <mono>next/font/google</mono> for self-hosted, FOUT-free loading:
typography.tailwindBody: Three Tailwind utilities map to the three families: <mono>font-display</mono>, <mono>font-body</mono>, <mono>font-mono</mono>.
typography.tailwindPreviewBody: Body copy uses the body family by default.
typography.helperBody: Three CSS classes ship with <mono>damo-ui/styles/globals.css</mono> for the most common Memphis-flavoured patterns.
typography.eyebrowPatternBody: Section headers in Damo UI pair an uppercase mono accent (the "eyebrow") with a display headline. The contrast in family + tracking is what gives the layout its Memphis edge.
typography.swapHint: Need to swap fonts entirely? Override <mono>--font-display</mono>, <mono>--font-body</mono>, <mono>--font-mono</mono> at <mono>:root</mono> — every utility and helper re-points automatically. See <link>Tokens</link> for the override pattern.

patterns.lead: Texture and shape primitives that fill empty space. Use one at a time — the Memphis look is loud enough that combining patterns turns into noise.
patterns.tilesBody: Every tile is a pure CSS gradient — there's no component needed. Apply the <mono>background</mono> shorthand directly on any element.
patterns.tilesCssTitle: Using the patterns directly in CSS
patterns.tilesCssBody: Drop the same <mono>background</mono> / <mono>background-image</mono> rules anywhere — works on any block element without React.
patterns.shapesBody: Eight Memphis-style SVG shapes: <mono>diamond</mono>, <mono>circle</mono>, <mono>triangle</mono>, <mono>zigzag</mono>, <mono>blob</mono>, <mono>wave</mono>, <mono>star</mono>, <mono>lbar</mono>. Pure SVG with <mono>aria-hidden</mono> — they're decoration only.

propsRows.color: Fill (or stroke for zigzag/wave). Any CSS color.

patterns.decorationBody: Combine <mono>absolute</mono> positioning with <mono>-z-10</mono> to float a shape behind a section title.
patterns.oneAtATimeBody: Memphis is loud. Stacking patterns kills hierarchy. Use one tile pattern + one shape accent at most per visual unit.
patterns.themingHint: Patterns reference the same semantic tokens as everything else, so they re-color when theme or palette changes — see <link>Theming</link>.

sidebar.subtitle: TOKEN EDITOR
sidebar.editingHeader: Editing the {mode} variant — values below apply when

includeOptions.tailwindFoundations: Foundations (typography / radius / shadow / spacing / motion / z-index)

paletteEditor.helpText: Editing raw scales does not automatically update the Theme tab values. Use Reset to re-derive semantic tokens from the current palette.

sampleDialog.title: Publish new release
sampleDialog.description: This is a sample modal. Surface, border, shadow, typography, and buttons follow the current theme generator tokens.
sampleDialog.helperText: You can change the tokens in the left panel: the modal above updates live, so you can verify how it appears with the theme applied.

accordion.lead: Stacked, expandable sections. Built on Radix Accordion — full keyboard wiring, controlled and uncontrolled modes, and the ARIA disclosure pattern come from the primitive.

a11y.0: Radix wraps each AccordionTrigger in an <h3> with a real <button> inside — aria-expanded tracks the open state and aria-controls wires it to the content panel.
a11y.1: Keyboard: <kbd>Tab</kbd> moves between triggers; <kbd>Enter</kbd> / <kbd>Space</kbd> toggle the focused panel; <kbd>Home</kbd> / <kbd>End</kbd> jump to the first / last trigger.

props.type: single allows one item open at a time; multiple allows several.
props.collapsible: Only valid for type="single". When true the active item can be closed by clicking it again.
props.onValueChange: Fires whenever the active item(s) change.

app-shell.lead: Two-column app layout: sticky sidebar (configurable width and tone) + scrollable main area. The shell takes the full viewport height and keeps the sidebar pinned while the main column scrolls.

body.basicShape: AppShell occupies the full viewport. The example below shows a static visual scaled into a framed preview — wire your own Sidebar (and <link1>PageHeader</link1>) inside the slots.
body.sidebarPlaceholder: Page content scrolls here. The sidebar stays pinned at the top.

a11y.0: <link1>Sidebar</link1> — composable side panel with header, body, footer slots.
a11y.1: <link1>PageHeader</link1> — eyebrow + title + actions strip for the top of the main column.
a11y.2: <link1>AppTopBar</link1> — alternative chrome for non-sidebar layouts (also can render inside the main column).

props.sidebar: Content rendered inside the sticky <aside> column. Pair with a Sidebar / Nav component.
props.sidebarWidth: Pixel width of the sidebar column.
props.sidebarTone: default paints the sidebar with the card surface; onDark flips it to ink with light text — pair with NavItem tone="onDark".
props.children: Main content rendered in the right column.

app-top-bar.lead: Site header with three slots: brand, navigation, and actions. Defaults to sticky top placement; opt out via sticky='{'false'}'.

a11y.0: The header renders as a <header> (banner role).
a11y.1: Wrap the brand mark in a link with an aria-label when the label is decorative (icon-only).

props.logo: Branding slot. Wrap a Next link or anchor that points to home.
props.nav: Inline navigation. Renders inside a <nav> element.
props.sticky: Stick the bar to the viewport top with z-index header.
props.className: Tailwind classes are merged on top of the defaults.

article-card.lead: Neutral content card capped at 420px wide. Memphis frame, optional eyebrow label, display-font title, and a relaxed-leading body.

aspect-ratio.lead: Locks a child element to a specific width-to-height ratio. Composes the Radix @radix-ui/react-aspect-ratio primitive — the wrapper guarantees no layout shift while the image / video / iframe loads.

a11y.0: 16 / 9 — widescreen video, hero banners
a11y.1: 4 / 3 — legacy media, photo galleries
a11y.2: 1 — square thumbnails, avatars

props.ratio: Width-to-height ratio (e.g. 16 / 9, 4 / 3, 1 for a square). The child element is forced to fill the box.
props.children: Pass a single element that should fill the aspect-ratio container.
props.className: Tailwind classes are merged on top of the wrapper.

attr-toggle-group.lead: A generic toggle bound to a data-* attribute on <html> and a localStorage key. It hydrates from storage on mount, sanitises stale persisted values back to the configured default, and offers two visual variants: a segmented button row or a Select dropdown. Use it to control any axis your product needs (theme, palette, density, language, currency, …) without coupling the component to a specific use case.

body.segmentedIntro: Default visual: a Memphis-bordered button row with aria-pressed on each option and a role="group" wrapper labelled by the eyebrow.
body.selectIntro: Use when the option list is too long for a button row, or when the surface is space-constrained.
body.headlessIntro: Drop the label prop when the surrounding UI already provides a heading.
body.sanitisationIntro: If a previously persisted value is no longer in options (e.g. you renamed a palette), the hook resets it to defaultValue on mount, then writes the corrected value back to localStorage and to the data-* attribute. The user never sees a flash of stale state.

a11y.0: The segmented variant wraps options in role="group"; when label is set, the group is labelled via aria-labelledby pointing to the eyebrow id.
a11y.1: Each segmented button is a native <button> with aria-pressed reflecting the active state.
a11y.2: The select variant exposes role="combobox" via the design-system Select primitive — full keyboard interaction is inherited.

props.options: List of '{' value, label '}' pairs the user can pick from.
props.storageKey: localStorage key that persists the active value across sessions.
props.attribute: data-* attribute mirrored on <html>. Must match ^data-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*$ — non-data attributes are rejected.
props.defaultValue: Initial value when nothing is persisted. Falls back to options[0].value.
props.label: Optional eyebrow label rendered before the control. When provided, the inner group is wired with aria-labelledby to the eyebrow id.
props.variant: segmented is a Memphis-bordered button row; select swaps to the design-system Select dropdown.
props.labelId: Override the auto-generated id for the eyebrow label. Use when you need to wire your own external label.
props.className: Tailwind classes merged on top of the wrapper defaults.

avatar.lead: User avatar with image + initial fallback. Built on Radix Avatar — when the image fails to load (or while loading), the fallback is shown instead.

body.groupIntro: Stack multiple avatars; max caps the visible count and renders a +N bubble for the rest.

a11y.0: Always pass alt on AvatarImage — for decorative avatars an empty string is acceptable; otherwise describe the user.
a11y.1: The fallback is purely visual — the alt text on the image (when present) carries the accessible name.

props.shape: square adds a 2px Memphis border and removes the rounding.

badge.lead: Compact uppercase label with a Memphis micro-shadow. Use to mark status, plan tier, or outcome on cards, table rows, and avatars. For longer labels with rounded corners reach for <link1>Chip</link1>.

a11y.0: Badge renders a <span> — the text content is the only thing announced. Pick labels that read meaningfully out of context (e.g. PRO rather than P).
a11y.1: When the badge encodes status that is not redundant with surrounding text (e.g. a single icon means "new"), pair it with a <span className="sr-only"> describing the meaning.

props.variant: Visual tone. featured uses the badge featured token; success / warning / info / destructive map to the standard status intents; outline strips the fill.
props.className: Tailwind classes are merged on top of the variant defaults.

banner.lead: Inline notification surface. Four tone variants (info, success, warning, danger), an optional title, an optional dismiss button, and an icon slot that defaults to a tone-aware glyph but accepts any ReactNode.

body.dismissible: Pass dismissible to add a close affordance. The banner clears its local state on click; pair with onDismiss to persist the dismissal (e.g. save to localStorage so it doesn't come back).
body.noIcon: Drop the leading icon when the banner is purely text-driven.

a11y.0: The wrapper renders role="status" for info, success and warning; role="alert" for danger.
a11y.1: Live-region note: alert and status only fire announcements when the banner is inserted into an already-mounted DOM. A banner present in the initial server render is read in document order, not as an interrupt — mount it dynamically (after a network response, after dismissing a modal, etc.) when you need the announcement.
a11y.2: Icons are aria-hidden — the title and body carry the message.
a11y.3: The dismiss button gets aria-label from dismissLabel (defaults to "Chiudi").

props.variant: Sets the background tint, the Memphis shadow color, and the default leading icon. danger also switches the wrapper role to alert for screen readers.
props.title: Optional bold heading rendered before the body.
props.icon: Override the variant default icon. Pass false to drop the icon entirely; pass any ReactNode to substitute a custom glyph.
props.dismissible: When true, renders an X button on the trailing edge that hides the banner from local state.
props.onDismiss: Fires when the user clicks the dismiss button. Use it to persist the dismissal.
props.dismissLabel: Aria label for the dismiss button.
props.children: Body content rendered below the title.

box.lead: Polymorphic flex primitive. Use it whenever you need a flex container with type-checked direction, gap, alignment and justification — and the option to render as any HTML element via as.

body.polymorphicIntro: Pass as to render Box as a different tag while keeping the layout utilities. Useful for landmark elements (<section>, <header>, <aside>) or to forward to a Next Link.

a11y.0: Most flex layouts in product code — replaces ad-hoc flex flex-row gap-3 items-center stacks.
a11y.1: For wider, page-level layouts with a max-width, prefer <link1>Container</link1>.
a11y.2: When the children must split into named regions, use a dedicated layout component such as <link1>AppShell</link1>.

props.as: Polymorphic element. Pass any HTML tag or component (section, header, aside, Link, …) and Box keeps its layout utilities while delegating semantics to the chosen element.
props.gap: Tailwind spacing step applied to the flex container gap.
props.inline: Switch to inline-flex when the box must sit inline with text.
props.className: Tailwind classes are merged on top of the variant defaults.

breadcrumbs.lead: Hierarchy trail rendered as a labelled <nav> landmark. The last item should be marked current so it renders as plain text with aria-current="page".

a11y.0: The wrapper renders <nav aria-label="Breadcrumb"> containing an ordered list — the canonical pattern for breadcrumb landmarks.
a11y.1: Separators are rendered as role="presentation" + aria-hidden, so screen readers announce only the item labels.
a11y.2: The current page should be the last item with current set — Radix renders it as a non-link <span> with aria-current="page".

props.separator: Custom node rendered between items. Defaults to a Memphis ChevronRightIcon. Override for a slash, dot, or any glyph.
props.className: Tailwind classes are merged on the wrapping <nav>.
props.href: Link target. Omit on the last (current) item.
props.current: Mark the item as the current page — renders a non-link <span> with aria-current="page".

button.lead: Bordered, shadowed action surface. Six variants, three sizes plus the dedicated icon size, full keyboard access via a native <button>. Pair with IconButton (documented below) for icon-only triggers — same chrome, locked to a 40×40 square.

body.withInlineIcons: Place an icon inside the Button alongside text. Keep the visible label so the action stays announce-able.
body.fullWidth: Stretch the button to the container — useful inside narrow forms or mobile sheets.
body.useAsLink: Pass asChild to render a single child element with all Button styles applied (Radix Slot pattern). Useful for navigation CTAs that need both the Memphis press animation and routing — the variant classes, hover lift, and active-press collapse are all merged onto the underlying <a>.
body.iconButtonLead: A square 40×40 button for icon-only actions. Composes Button with size="icon" and the same Memphis press effect; aria-label is required so the action is announced.
body.iconButtonVariants: Same six variants as Button. The icon-only version of link is rarely useful; reach for ghost instead.

a11y.0: Renders a native <button type="button">; pass type="submit" when used inside a form.
a11y.1: Always provide a non-empty label. For icon-only triggers use IconButton (documented below) with an aria-label.
a11y.2: disabled blocks both pointer and keyboard activation on a real <button>. With asChild, only the disabled CSS utilities apply — for a fully inactive link also remove href or set aria-disabled="true" on the child.

iconButtonA11y.0: aria-label is required and must be non-empty. The TypeScript signature enforces this — passing aria-label="" is a type error.
iconButtonA11y.1: The icon child is rendered visually only; ensure the surrounding context doesn't rely on the icon being announced (it is not).
iconButtonA11y.2: The 40×40 square satisfies WCAG 2.5.8 (Level AA, WCAG 2.2), which requires a 24×24 CSS pixel minimum. To reach the 44×44 threshold of WCAG 2.5.5 (Level AAA), wrap the button with a 4px margin or add hit-slop on the parent.

props.variant: Visual style. Primary/secondary use the brand accents; outline and ghost are neutral; destructive surfaces destructive intent; link reduces to an inline anchor.
props.size: Adjusts vertical padding and font size. icon is a 40×40 square — used internally by IconButton.
props.disabled: On a real <button>, blocks pointer / keyboard activation. With asChild on a non-button child, only the disabled CSS utilities apply — set aria-disabled and remove href on the child for full inertness.
props.fullWidth: Stretches the button to the full width of its container.
props.asChild: When true, render the child element with all Button styles applied (Radix Slot pattern). The child must be a single React element such as a <Link> or <a>.
props.className: Tailwind classes are merged on top of the variant defaults.

iconButtonProps.ariaLabel: IconButtons have no visible text, so every instance MUST carry a non-empty aria-label for screen readers. The TypeScript signature enforces this.
iconButtonProps.children: A single icon node — typically one of the icons exported from damo-ui.
iconButtonProps.variant: Same six variants as Button. The size is locked to the 40×40 icon preset.

card.lead: Composable surface container. Five variants share the same Memphis chrome but differ in accent, weight, and elevation.

body.elevatedIntro: Soft drop shadow rather than the hard Memphis shadow. Good for content that should sit behind primary surfaces.
body.inverseIntro: Flipped bg/fg — use on a light page when you need a section to feel like a dark island.

props.variant: Visual treatment. Featured uses the gold accent; inverse flips fg/bg for dark surfaces.
props.padding: Inner spacing applied to the body and chrome rows.
props.className: Tailwind classes are merged on top of the variant defaults.

checkbox.lead: Memphis-bordered checkbox built on Radix Checkbox. Supports tri-state checked / unchecked / indeterminate, plus all native form semantics (name, value, required).

a11y.0: Radix Checkbox renders role="checkbox" with aria-checked reflecting the checked state, including "mixed" for the indeterminate case.
a11y.1: Always pair with a Label wired via htmlFor, or supply aria-label when there is no visible text.
a11y.2: Native keyboard support inherited: <kbd>Space</kbd> toggles the checked state.

props.onCheckedChange: Fires whenever the checked state changes.
props.required: Marks the input as required for native form submission.
props.name: Name attribute for native form submission.

chip.lead: Pill-shaped tag with optional status dot and active state. Use chips for filters, tags, and soft status labels. For uppercase Memphis-shadowed badges (status / outcome), reach for <link1>Badge</link1>.

a11y.0: Chip renders a <span> — when used inside a clickable filter, wrap it in a <button> with aria-pressed reflecting the active state.
a11y.1: The status dot is purely decorative; the label text must convey the same status (do not rely on color alone).

props.variant: Background tint and border color. All variants share the rounded pill shape.
props.dotColor: When set, prepends an 8×8 dot before children. Accepts any CSS color string (var(--success), #16a34a, etc.).
props.active: Swap the surface to the primary token (selected look). With dotColor the dot border flips to white for contrast.
props.children: Chip label — supports text or icon + text.

color-picker.lead: Native <input type="color"> swatch paired with the design-system Input for a hex string. Both controls share a single value / onChange — the parent decides whether to validate the hex.

body.hideVisibleLabel: aria-label is preserved on both controls, so the component remains accessible even when the visible label is hidden.

props.label: Visible label rendered above the controls. Also used as the aria-label on the swatch + hex input.
props.value: Current color string. Pass any value the consumer expects (typically a hex).
props.onChange: Fires whenever the swatch or the hex input emits a new value. Validate the string before re-binding it to state.
props.showInput: Hide the hex text input and show only the color swatch.
props.showLabel: Hide the visible label — aria-label is still wired for screen readers.
props.id: Override the auto-generated id used to wire the label and the color input.

combobox.lead: Searchable single-select. Trigger looks like Select; the dropdown content uses cmdk for fuzzy matching plus the Damo Memphis chrome on the popover.

body.controlled: Manage the selection externally for validation or persistence.
body.nativeFormSubmission: Combobox renders a <button> + popover with no hidden form input and no name prop. To submit through a traditional <form>, manage the value with React state and either render your own hidden input or read it from the submit handler.

vsSelect.0: Reach for Combobox when the option list is long enough that scrolling alone is friction (≥ ~10 options) or when users will search by typed input.
vsSelect.1: Reach for <link1>Select</link1> for short lists (2-9 items) where keyboard arrow navigation is sufficient.

a11y.0: The trigger is a plain <button> (no role="combobox") with aria-expanded reflecting the popover state. This is a button-plus-popover pattern, not the full WAI-ARIA combobox pattern that Select provides.
a11y.1: The dropdown list is rendered by cmdk: type to filter, arrows to move, Enter to select, Esc to close.
a11y.2: Use FormField or pair id with a sibling Label so screen readers announce the field name.

props.options: Items to render — '{' value, label, disabled? '}'.
props.onValueChange: Fires when an option is picked.
props.placeholder: Trigger text shown when nothing is selected.
props.emptyMessage: Shown when the search yields no results.
props.id: id forwarded to the trigger button (label association).
props.className: Tailwind classes merged onto the trigger.

container.lead: Centred max-width column with responsive horizontal padding. The default page wrapper for Damo UI documents.

body.sizeVariants: Six size steps mirror Tailwind's screen-* breakpoints, plus a full option for full-bleed sections.
body.unpadded: Disable padding when the children already manage their own gutters or for full-bleed hero banners.

props.size: Maximum width breakpoint. full removes the max-width entirely (useful for full-bleed banners that still want centred padding).
props.padded: When true, applies responsive horizontal padding (px-4 md:px-6 lg:px-8).
props.className: Tailwind classes are merged on top of the defaults.

context-menu.lead: Right-click menu built on Radix ContextMenu. Mirrors DropdownMenu in API and item types — the difference is the trigger: any wrapped element opens on contextmenu events (right click, long press on touch, Shift-F10).

a11y.0: Radix wires role="menu" + role="menuitem" the same way DropdownMenu does, including support for checkbox / radio items.
a11y.1: Keyboard: <kbd>Shift</kbd> + <kbd>F10</kbd> opens the menu from the focused trigger. Arrows cycle items; <kbd>Esc</kbd> closes.

whenToUse.0: File managers, table rows, canvas elements — anywhere users expect a right-click menu of contextual actions.
whenToUse.1: Don't replace primary affordances with ContextMenu only — touch users on iOS discover them via long-press, but Android contexts are spotty. Always provide an equivalent action via a button or DropdownMenu.

props.modal: Trap focus while the menu is open. Set false to allow interaction with the rest of the page.
props.onOpenChange: Fires when the menu opens or closes.

date-picker.lead: Single-date picker that pairs a Memphis-styled trigger with the react-day-picker calendar inside a Popover. Locale defaults to Italian; pass any DayPicker prop through to override.

body.controlled: Manage the selection externally to react to changes (validation, persistence, syncing across fields).
body.nativeFormSubmission: DatePicker renders a <button> + popover, not a native <input type="date">, and ships no hidden form input. To submit the value through a traditional <form>, manage the date with React state and either render your own hidden input or post the value via FormData in the submit handler.

a11y.0: The calendar inherits react-day-picker's ARIA wiring (grid + day cells with aria-selected).
a11y.1: Wrap with FormField (or supply your own label) so the field is announced to screen readers.
a11y.2: Keyboard: Space/Enter opens the popover, arrow keys navigate days, Esc closes it.

props.onValueChange: Fires when the user picks a day. Receives undefined when the date is cleared.
props.placeholder: Trigger text shown when no date is selected.
props.formatStr: date-fns format string used for the trigger label.
props.id: Forwarded to the trigger button (useful for label association).
props.className: Class name on the wrapper div.
props.triggerClassName: Class name on the trigger button itself.

dialog.lead: Modal dialog wrapping Radix Dialog. Two semantic modes via severity: default (informational, overlay closes) and alert (destructive confirmation, overlay does NOT close). A separate tone prop flips the offset shadow to the destructive token.

headings.whenToSwitchToAlert: When to switch to alert mode

body.defaultInformational: Standard modal. Click on the overlay or press <kbd>Esc</kbd> to dismiss.
body.alertSeverity: Flip the role to alertdialog, block overlay-click dismissal, and remove the X button — the user must explicitly choose an action. Pair with tone="danger" to recolour the Memphis shadow.

whenToAlert.0: The action is destructive (delete, archive, sign out) and must NOT be cancelled accidentally by clicking outside.
whenToAlert.1: You want screen readers to announce the dialog as alertdialog (more interruptive than the regular dialog role).

a11y.0: Always pair DialogTitle with the content; screen readers announce it as the dialog name.
a11y.1: Add DialogDescription for non-obvious actions; it is wired as aria-describedby.
a11y.2: Use DialogClose on close buttons so they close the dialog and return focus to the trigger.
a11y.3: In severity="alert", place Cancel first in the footer DOM order so Radix gives it default focus — the layout enforces column-reverse on small viewports so Cancel sits on top there.

props.onOpenChange: Fires whenever the open state changes.
props.modal: When true (default), focus is trapped inside the dialog and outside content is inert.

contentProps.severity: default is role="dialog"; clicks on the overlay close it. alert flips to role="alertdialog", blocks overlay-click dismissal and hides the X button — the user must click an explicit footer action. Use for destructive or otherwise irreversible flows.
contentProps.tone: Visual override. danger swaps the Memphis offset shadow to the destructive token, telegraphing high-stakes actions. Orthogonal to severity — combine freely.
contentProps.hideClose: Hide the built-in X close button. Already implicit when severity="alert" — alert mode never renders the X.

drawer.lead: Sliding side panel built on the Radix Dialog primitive. Comes in four sides (left, right, top, bottom), with a close button baked in and the same Memphis shadow chrome as Dialog.

body.sides: Right is the default — typical for filters and detail panels. Bottom works well on mobile.

a11y.0: Built on Radix Dialog: focus is trapped inside the panel while open and returned to the trigger on close. Esc closes the drawer; clicks on the overlay close it too.
a11y.1: Always pair DrawerTitle and DrawerDescription with the content — Radix wires them as the dialog's accessible name and description.
a11y.2: The built-in close button uses an Italian label aria-label="Chiudi". There is currently no prop to override it; if you need a different language, set hideClose and render your own close affordance with DrawerClose as asChild.

vsDialog.0: Reach for Drawer when the panel hosts a flow of related controls (filters, settings, side editor) — the panel can stay tall and dominate one edge of the screen.
vsDialog.1: Reach for <link1>Dialog</link1> for a centred modal — confirmations, single-step forms, or focused tasks.

props.open: Controlled open state on the root.
props.defaultOpen: Uncontrolled initial open state on the root.
props.onOpenChange: Fires whenever the open state changes.
props.side: Prop on DrawerContent. Sets the slide-in edge of the panel.
props.hideClose: Prop on DrawerContent. Hides the built-in close button (rendered as an X in the upper right). Useful when the consumer renders their own close affordance.

dropdown-menu.lead: Composable action menu built on Radix DropdownMenu. Memphis-bordered surface, full keyboard wiring (Enter / Space, arrows, Esc, type-ahead), and selection chrome on radio items that mirrors the <link1>NavItem</link1> gradient. For a non-menu floating panel (free-form preferences, an editor popover, custom chrome), reach for <link2>Popover</link2> instead.

body.mixedItemTypes: DropdownMenu also exposes DropdownMenuCheckboxItem and DropdownMenuRadioGroup + DropdownMenuRadioItem when the menu drives state instead of firing actions.

a11y.0: Radix wires the ARIA Menu pattern: trigger gets aria-haspopup + aria-expanded; items get role="menuitem" / menuitemcheckbox / menuitemradio.
a11y.1: Keyboard: <kbd>Enter</kbd> / <kbd>Space</kbd> open the menu and activate items, arrows cycle, <kbd>Esc</kbd> closes, type-ahead jumps to the first item starting with the typed string.

comparison.0: DropdownMenu opens on click and is the standard "more actions" affordance.
comparison.1: <link1>ContextMenu</link1> opens on right-click of an arbitrary surface (table rows, canvas, file lists).
comparison.2: <link2>Popover</link2> is for free-form floating content (forms, search, help) — not a menu.

props.open: Controlled open state on the root.
props.onOpenChange: Fires when the menu opens or closes.
props.modal: Prop on the root. Default modal traps focus. Set false to allow interaction with the rest of the page while open.
props.align: Prop on DropdownMenuContent. Inline alignment relative to the trigger.
props.side: Prop on DropdownMenuContent. Preferred edge to anchor against.

feature-card.lead: Compact 280px feature/highlight card with a primary-tinted Memphis shadow. Use to surface product features on landing pages or in feature grids.

props.title: Display-font title.
props.icon: Optional icon rendered next to the meta.

form-field.lead: Accessible wrapper that wires a label, description, and error message to a single input child via aria-describedby and aria-invalid. Ships zero opinions about layout — pair it with Box for stacks.

body.errorState: Pass an error string to surface a validation message. The wrapper sets aria-invalid on the child and links the error to aria-describedby.

a11y.0: The label is a real <label htmlFor="…">; clicking it focuses the input.
a11y.1: Description and error ids are merged into the child's aria-describedby; existing ids passed by the caller are preserved.
a11y.2: When error is set, the child receives aria-invalid=true automatically.

props.label: Visible label rendered as a <label> and wired to the child via htmlFor.
props.description: Optional helper text. The id is added to the child via aria-describedby.
props.error: Optional validation message. When present, it is added to aria-describedby and the child also receives aria-invalid=true.
props.id: Override the auto-generated id. Useful when you need to reference the field from outside the form.
props.children: Exactly one child element. The wrapper clones it and injects id, aria-describedby, and aria-invalid.
props.labelClassName: Customise the label classes (e.g. to hide for visually-hidden labels).

hint.lead: Numbered Memphis callout. Use to highlight steps in a how-to, key insights inside long-form docs, or numbered captions on a marketing page.

body.accessibility: Hint renders a vanilla div with semantic h4 + p children. The number tile carries no aria-hidden, so screen readers will announce it before the title — this is intentional, since the number is part of the callout's meaning.

props.num: Number rendered inside the leading 40×40 tile.
props.title: Short heading rendered in display font.
props.className: Tailwind classes are merged on top of the wrapper.

input.lead: Single-line text field. Native <input> with the Memphis 2-px border and focus shadow. Pairs with Label for accessibility.

a11y.0: Always associate a Label via matching htmlFor / id — placeholders are not labels.
a11y.1: Use invalid together with aria-describedby pointing at an error message for screen readers.

props.invalid: Marks the input as invalid (sets aria-invalid + applies the destructive shadow).
props.disabled: Disables the input and applies the muted background.
props.rest: All native input attributes are forwarded.

label.lead: Native <label> with the form-label typography baked in (text-xs font-semibold uppercase tracking-wider text-muted-foreground). Use it standalone when you don't need the structured wiring of FormField.

body.whenFormField: For most product forms, FormField is preferable: it auto-wires htmlFor, aria-describedby, and aria-invalid based on description / error props. Reach for the standalone Label only when you have a one-off layout that needs custom positioning.

props.htmlFor: Standard <label> association — when set, clicking the label focuses the field with the matching id.
props.className: Tailwind classes are merged on top of the typography defaults.

medal.lead: Heptagonal medal SVG with a five-rank token palette. Use it to render leaderboard ranks, achievement badges, and rating tiers.

body.accessibility: The SVG renders role="img" with aria-label derived from label (or a default like "gold medal" when no label is provided).

props.rank: Selects the inner / outer / text token (--medal-'{'rank'}'-'{'outer|inner|text'}').
props.label: Mono uppercase caption rendered below the medal.
props.size: SVG width and height in pixels.

memphis-shape.lead: Inline SVG shape primitives used as decorative accents on landing pages, hero sections, and the design system showcase. Eight variants share a 100×100 viewBox so they scale cleanly.

body.accessibility: MemphisShape SVGs are rendered with aria-hidden="true" — purely decorative. If you need a meaningful shape (e.g. an icon button), use one of the components in the icon set and supply aria-label on the wrapper.

props.variant: Shape to render. Filled variants accept a fill; stroke variants accept a stroke.
props.size: Pixel size applied to both width and height of the SVG.
props.color: Fill (or stroke for outline variants). Accepts any CSS color string.

nav-item.lead: Sidebar-row primitive used by the Damo UI docs nav and any sidebar that needs the unified selection chrome (gradient + 1px inset outline + 3px accent bar). Polymorphic by default via as.

body.polymorphic: Pass any element via as. NavItem forwards aria-current when active is true regardless of the rendered tag.

a11y.0: When active is set, NavItem applies aria-current="page" — the canonical signal for "you are here" in a navigation list.
a11y.1: Default render is <a>; for client-side routing pass as='{'Link'}' (Next) or your router's link component. The selection chrome and class merging are preserved.

props.as: Polymorphic element. Pass Link, button, or any component to control routing semantics.
props.active: When true, applies the selection chrome (gradient + outline + accent bar) and sets aria-current="page".
props.icon: Optional leading icon rendered inside a 20×20 slot.
props.endAdornment: Optional trailing element — typically a Badge marking new / beta / count.
props.tone: default is the standard light/dark sidebar tone; onDark swaps the muted text and selection colors to the on-dark navbar palette (used inside AppTopBar).

ornament.lead: Decorative section break: two horizontal gradient hairlines flanking a centred glyph. Use between thematic groups inside a page where a plain Separator would feel too clinical.

body.customGlyph: Pass a child node to replace the default diamond — emojis, icons, or short text accents work well.
body.accessibility: The wrapper carries aria-hidden="true", which removes Ornament from the accessibility tree entirely — assistive technology skips it. The DOM role="separator" attribute is present for styling consistency but has no AT effect because of the surrounding aria-hidden. For semantic section breaks that should be announced, use Separator with decorative=false.

props.children: Optional glyph rendered between the two gradient hairlines. Defaults to a Memphis diamond.
props.className: Tailwind classes are merged on top of the wrapper.

page-header.lead: Top-of-page strip with eyebrow + display title + description + right-aligned actions slot. Renders a real <header> landmark with a bottom hairline border.

a11y.0: The wrapper is a real <header> — adds a banner landmark when used at the page level.
a11y.1: The title renders as <h1>; only one PageHeader per route.

props.eyebrow: Optional small uppercase label rendered above the title.
props.title: Page heading rendered as an <h1> in the display font.
props.actions: Optional right-aligned slot for primary actions (Buttons, IconButtons, …).

pagination.lead: Numbered page navigator with prev / next chevrons, ellipsis collapsing on long ranges, and localizable labels. The page-window math is exposed as computePageWindow if you need to compose your own UI.

body.longRange: With many pages, Pagination shows the first / last / current window and inserts ellipsis between gaps.

a11y.0: Wrapper is <nav aria-label="Pagination">.
a11y.1: The current page button gets aria-current="page"; all other page buttons get an aria-label like Pagina 3 (localised via labels.page).
a11y.2: Ellipses render aria-hidden — they are not announced.
a11y.3: Keyboard: page numbers and the prev/next chevrons are native <button> elements — Tab moves focus across them, Enter / Space activate them. Disabled buttons (prev at page 1, next at the last page) are skipped from the focus order automatically.

props.currentPage: Active page (1-indexed). Drives aria-current on the matching button.
props.maxVisible: Maximum number of page buttons rendered between the ellipses. Defaults to a sensible window via computePageWindow (also exported).
props.onPageChange: Fires when the user clicks a page number or the prev/next chevrons.
props.labels: Override accessible labels — '{' previous, next, page, pageOf(page, total) '}'. Defaults are English.
props.disabled: Disables every button (useful while a parent fetch is in flight).

popover.lead: Non-modal floating panel anchored to a trigger. Built on Radix Popover, painted with the same Memphis chrome as DropdownMenu and Dialog so floating surfaces feel consistent. Use it as the generic container for preferences, mini-editors, search results, or any non-menu floating content. For an action-list with the full ARIA menu pattern reach for DropdownMenu.

body.alignment: Use align for inline alignment and side / sideOffset for the anchor edge.

vsList.0: Popover — a free-form floating panel. Use it for forms, search results, help text, mini-editors.
vsList.1: DropdownMenu — a list of actions with full keyboard wiring (arrow keys cycle items). Reach for it whenever the content is a menu.
vsList.2: <link1>Dialog</link1> — a modal centred on the screen. Reach for it when the user must finish a task before continuing.

a11y.0: Trigger inherits Radix aria-expanded / aria-controls.
a11y.1: Default modal=false — focus is not trapped, and clicks outside dismiss the panel. Set modal=true when the popover hosts a complete task that should block other interactions.
a11y.2: Use PopoverClose on close affordances inside the content so focus returns to the trigger.

props.open: Controlled open state on the root.
props.defaultOpen: Uncontrolled initial open state on the root.
props.onOpenChange: Fires when the open state changes.
props.modal: When true, focus is trapped while open and outside content is inert. Default is false (non-modal).
props.align: Prop on PopoverContent. Alignment of the content relative to the trigger.
props.side: Prop on PopoverContent. Preferred edge to anchor against. Radix flips automatically when there is no room.

progress.lead: Determinate progress bar built on Radix Progress. Memphis-bordered track with a secondary token fill that animates to value.

body.customIndicator: Override the fill bar with indicatorClassName — useful for success / warning / danger flows.
body.indeterminate: Pass value='{'null'}' when total length is unknown — Radix sets aria-busy=true. Reach for Spinner for compact indeterminate feedback.

a11y.0: Radix sets role="progressbar" with aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, and aria-valuenow.
a11y.1: Always supply aria-label or aria-labelledby describing what the bar represents (Uploading file, Profile completion…).

props.value: Current progress 0..100. Pass null for an indeterminate state (Radix marks it aria-busy).
props.max: Maximum value forwarded to Radix Progress.
props.indicatorClassName: Tailwind classes applied to the inner indicator (the filling bar).
props.className: Tailwind classes merged on top of the track.
props.ariaLabel: Required when no surrounding visible label is present.

radio-group.lead: Mutually-exclusive radio group built on Radix RadioGroup. Wraps each option in a Memphis-bordered button with a filled inner dot when selected. Use it whenever the user must pick exactly one of three or more options; for two-option toggles reach for <link1>SegmentedControl</link1> or <link2>Switch</link2>.

a11y.0: Radix wires role="radiogroup" on the root and role="radio" on each item with proper aria-checked.
a11y.1: Pair each RadioGroupItem with a Label wired via htmlFor so the click area extends to the label.
a11y.2: Keyboard: Tab moves focus to the currently selected item (roving tabindex); arrow keys cycle items and change the selection; Tab again moves focus out of the group.

props.name: Name attribute for native form submission.
props.disabled: Disables every item in the group.
props.required: Marks the group as required for native form submission.

scroll-area.lead: Custom-styled scroll container that replaces native scrollbars with a Memphis-themed track and thumb. Composes @radix-ui/react-scroll-area; both vertical and horizontal scrollbars are wired automatically.

body.horizontal: ScrollArea wires both vertical and horizontal scrollbars; let the inner content overflow horizontally to surface the horizontal bar.

a11y.0: Bounded panels with overflowing children (file lists, chat threads, tag carousels).
a11y.1: When you need consistent scrollbar styling across browsers — native bars vary heavily on Safari / Windows / Linux.
a11y.2: Skip ScrollArea for the document scroll itself — let the browser handle the page-level scroll for accessibility.

props.children: Scrollable content — wrapped in a Radix viewport that handles overflow.
props.className: Tailwind classes for the outer wrapper. Apply height / width here so overflow is bounded.
props.type: Forwarded to Radix Root. Controls when scrollbars are visible — hover reveals them on pointer hover, always keeps them in place.

segmented-control.lead: Single-select toggle group with a Memphis border seam between items. Built on Radix ToggleGroup with type="single". Use it for 2-5 mutually exclusive options where keeping the choices visible adds clarity.

a11y.0: The root renders role="group" with each item as role="radio" + aria-checked (Radix ToggleGroup type="single" uses the radio pattern under the hood).
a11y.1: aria-label (or aria-labelledby) on the root is required: without it the group has no accessible name, and screen readers will announce the items without context.
a11y.2: Keyboard: <kbd>Tab</kbd> moves focus into the currently selected item (roving tabindex); arrow keys cycle items and move the selection; <kbd>Tab</kbd> again moves focus out of the entire group.

vsRadio.0: Reach for SegmentedControl when 2-5 options fit on one line and the control acts more like a tab strip than a form input.
vsRadio.1: Reach for <link1>RadioGroup</link1> when you have many options or want each option on its own line with descriptions.

props.ariaLabel: Required when there is no surrounding visible label.
props.disabled: Disables every item in the group.

select.lead: Composable dropdown built on Radix Select. Same Memphis border + focus shadow as <link1>Input</link1>. For multi-select or fuzzy search, reach for <link2>Combobox</link2>.

body.grouped: Wrap related items in SelectGroup with a non-interactive SelectLabel. Use SelectSeparator between groups.

a11y.0: Trigger renders role="combobox" with aria-expanded and aria-controls.
a11y.1: Inherits Radix Select keyboard support: type-ahead, arrow navigation, Home/End, Esc to close.
a11y.2: Provide a label for the trigger via FormField or aria-labelledby when no visible label exists.

props.name: Native form name.
props.open: Controlled open state of the dropdown. Pair with onOpenChange.
props.onOpenChange: Fires whenever the dropdown opens or closes.

separator.lead: Horizontal or vertical divider with three Memphis-aware variants. Composes the Radix Separator primitive, so its semantics flip between decorative and meaningful via decorative.

a11y.0: Defaults to decorative=true — no role is announced. Use this for purely visual separation.
a11y.1: Set decorative=false when the divider represents meaningful structure (e.g. between thematic page sections); Radix exposes role="separator" with the right aria-orientation.

props.variant: solid is a single hairline; dashed switches to a dashed border; memphis-double is a double-bordered slab matching the Memphis aesthetic.
props.decorative: When true (default), the separator is purely decorative and hidden from screen readers. Pass false when the divider conveys meaningful structure that should be announced.
props.className: Tailwind classes are merged on top of the variant defaults.

sidebar.lead: Composable side panel with header, body, and footer slots. Used here on the docs site for the navigation rail you see to the left.

props.ariaLabel: Required when the sidebar is the only nav landmark on the page.
props.className: Tailwind classes are merged on top of the defaults.

skeleton.lead: Shimmering placeholder block to keep layout stable while async content loads. Mark up the skeleton with the same dimensions as the eventual content to avoid layout shift on swap-in.

a11y.0: Skeleton sets aria-hidden="true" — it is purely visual and skipped by screen readers.
a11y.1: When the content arrives, swap the skeleton out atomically. Long-running loads should surface progress via role="status" (use Spinner or a hidden status text) so the change is announced.

props.className: Required: shape the skeleton with width / height / radius utilities. The base Skeleton ships only the shimmer animation and muted background.

slider.lead: Memphis-bordered slider with one or more thumbs. Built on Radix Slider — full keyboard support, drag handling, and ARIA semantics come from the primitive.

body.rangeIntro: Pass two values to render a range with a primary fill between them.
body.verticalIntro: Wrap the Slider in a container with an explicit height — the vertical orientation makes the Slider fill its parent's height.

a11y.0: Each thumb is a role="slider" with aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, and aria-valuenow.
a11y.1: Provide context with aria-label or aria-labelledby — what does the value represent?
a11y.2: Keyboard: arrows step by step, Home/End jump to min/max, PageUp/PageDown move by 10× step.

props.onValueChange: Fires while dragging or with keyboard arrows.
props.orientation: Drag axis. Vertical sliders need an explicit height on the wrapper.
props.inverted: Reverse the visual direction (right-to-left or top-to-bottom).

spinner.lead: Lightweight SVG spinner with an accessible role="status" announcement. Use for compact, indeterminate loading states inside buttons, list rows or tooltips. For full bars reach for Progress.

body.colorOverride: Spinner uses currentColor — change the wrapper's text color to retint.

a11y.0: Renders role="status" with the supplied label as aria-label.
a11y.1: For inline use inside a labelled button, set label="" and rely on the button's own text — avoid duplicate announcements.

props.size: Width and height of the SVG. Pass any number (px) or CSS unit string.
props.label: Accessible name announced by screen readers (aria-label). Localize for non-Italian UIs.
props.className: Tailwind classes are merged on top of the spin animation + primary color.

stat.lead: KPI block: mono uppercase label, big display-font value, optional delta line. Use to surface key metrics in dashboards and overview pages.

props.label: Mono uppercase label rendered above the value.
props.value: Headline number rendered in the display font.
props.delta: Optional sub-line showing the change vs a baseline.
props.deltaTone: Color of the delta line — success / destructive / muted.
props.icon: Optional icon rendered beside the label.

switch.lead: Memphis toggle: square thumb sliding between an ivory and a primary track. Use for boolean preferences that take effect immediately (notifications on / off, dark mode on / off). Built on Radix Switch.

body.vsCheckbox0: Use Switch when toggling a setting takes effect immediately (push notifications, dark mode).
body.vsCheckbox1: Use <link1>Checkbox</link1> when the value is part of a form that gets submitted (accept terms, select preferences).

a11y.0: Renders role="switch" with aria-checked reflecting on/off state.
a11y.1: Always associate a visible label or pass aria-label.
a11y.2: Keyboard: <kbd>Space</kbd> toggles.

props.onCheckedChange: Fires whenever the checked state changes.
props.name: Name attribute for native form submission.
props.value: Submitted form value when checked. Defaults to "on".

table.lead: Memphis-bordered table primitives. Wrap rows in semantic <thead> / <tbody> / <tfoot> sections via the matching components — the outer container handles overflow + borders.

a11y.0: Always include TableCaption when the table conveys data — screen readers announce the caption as the table's name.
a11y.1: Each TableHead renders a real <th>; for row-headers add scope="row".
a11y.2: For very large tables consider pairing with <link1>Pagination</link1> and a virtualised body.

props.TableCaption: Optional caption rendered below the table.
props.TableHead: Column header cell — uppercase mono text.
props.TableCell: Body cell — pad px-4 py-3 by default.

tabs.lead: Inline tab strip + matching content panels. Built on Radix Tabs — full keyboard wiring, ARIA semantics, and roving tabindex come from the primitive. Active tab is marked with a primary-colored 3px underline.

body.vsSegmented: Reach for <link1>SegmentedControl</link1> when the user is filtering or toggling visibility within the same panel; reach for Tabs when each value owns a meaningfully distinct content region with its own ARIA tab panel.

a11y.0: Radix wires the ARIA Tabs pattern: role="tablist", role="tab", and role="tabpanel" with aria-controls / aria-labelledby linking them.
a11y.1: Keyboard: Tab moves into the tab strip; arrow keys cycle tabs; Home/End jump to the first/last tab. With activationMode="manual" the focused tab is activated only on Enter/Space.

props.onValueChange: Fires when the active tab changes.
props.orientation: Layout direction for keyboard navigation (arrow key axis).
props.activationMode: automatic activates a tab on focus; manual requires Enter/Space — useful when switching tabs is expensive.

textarea.lead: Multi-line text input with the same Memphis border, hover, and focus chrome as <link1>Input</link1>. Resizable vertically by default; pair with <link2>FormField</link2> for label + description + error wiring.

a11y.0: Renders a native <textarea>; native form submission and validation work as expected.
a11y.1: Pair with FormField (or supply your own aria-labelledby / aria-describedby) so screen readers announce label, description, and error.

props.invalid: When true, sets aria-invalid and switches the border + Memphis shadow to the destructive token.
props.disabled: Disables the field. The Memphis hover/focus styles also drop.
props.className: Tailwind classes are merged on top of the defaults.

toast.lead: Transient notification that slides in from the screen edge. Built on Radix Toast — full ARIA live-region wiring, swipe-to-dismiss, and pause-on-hover come from the primitive. Use for non-blocking feedback (saved, copied, sent); for critical errors that need acknowledgement use <link1>Dialog with severity="alert"</link1>.

body.providerIntro: Mount one ToastProvider + ToastViewport at the root of your app. All toasts emitted anywhere in the tree render inside the viewport.
body.variantsIntro: Click each button to slide the matching toast into the bottom-right of its preview frame. Real Toast instances mounted inside a scoped ToastProvider + ToastViewport — same wiring you'd ship in production.
body.emittingIntro: Toasts in Radix are state-driven: render a <Toast /> with controlled open and toggle it from your event handlers. For app-wide toast emission, build a thin context that owns the queue.

a11y.0: The viewport sets role="region" with aria-label="Notifications". Toasts inside render with appropriate role + aria-live per Radix.
a11y.1: When using ToastAction, supply altText — the accessibility name read aloud when the toast appears. Without it Radix throws.
a11y.2: Hovering or focusing the viewport pauses the auto-dismiss timer; <kbd>F6</kbd> jumps focus into the viewport from anywhere on the page.

props.variant: Sets the background tint and Memphis shadow color. Mirrors the variant tokens used by Banner.
props.onOpenChange: Fires when the toast opens or closes.
props.duration: Auto-dismiss timeout in milliseconds. Pass Infinity to disable.
props.altText: Prop on ToastAction. Required by Radix when the trigger has no text — used as the accessible action name.

tooltip.lead: Small floating label revealed on hover or focus. Built on Radix Tooltip — full keyboard support, smart edge flipping, and ARIA wiring come from the primitive. Wrap your app once in TooltipProvider to share a single delay timer across all tooltips.

body.providerIntro: Wrap your root layout in a single TooltipProvider. All nested tooltips share its delay. Without the provider, individual tooltips still work but don't coordinate timing.
body.iconButtonIntro: Pair Tooltip with IconButton to surface the action's name on hover. aria-label on the IconButton stays the canonical accessible name; the tooltip is supplementary.

a11y.0: The trigger receives Radix focus management; pressing <kbd>Tab</kbd> opens the tooltip, <kbd>Esc</kbd> closes it.
a11y.1: Tooltips don't replace labels. For icon-only triggers, always set aria-label on the trigger — the tooltip text alone is not announced as the accessible name.
a11y.2: Tooltip content is not focusable. Use <link1>Popover</link1> when the user must interact with the floating panel.

props.delayDuration: Prop on TooltipProvider (and per-Tooltip). Milliseconds before the tooltip opens on hover.
props.side: Prop on TooltipContent. Preferred edge to anchor against; flips automatically.
props.align: Inline alignment relative to the trigger.

user-card.lead: Horizontal user row with avatar + name + optional meta and trailing slots. Memphis frame with a 4px shadow.

props.name: Display name. Used to derive the default avatar initial.
props.avatar: Custom avatar node. When omitted UserCard renders a circular ink tile with the first letter of name.
props.meta: Optional mono caption rendered under the name.
props.trailing: Optional right-aligned slot — typically a Badge or status chip.
